qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default localhost API is browser-readable and browser-writable through wildcard CORS and optional auth
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding identifies the same src/server/http.ts root cause as the advisory: fail-open auth when ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is unset, wildcard CORS on JSON and OPTIONS responses, and browser-writable /learn on the localhost HTTP server.

selected_finding.title: Default localhost API is browser-readable and browser-writable through wildcard CORS and optional auth

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's vulnerable localhost HTTP server logic in src/server/http.ts, and the copied PoC run demonstrated both anonymous cross-origin graph data read and persistent attacker-controlled /learn write under wildcard CORS.
evidence: At commit 74c1e61040ff125124adbe8c99bf3fe5a6b5df20, /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_2c389da7/.judge/reference/engram/src/server/http.ts implements wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin in json() and OPTIONS handling and returns true from checkAuth() whenever ENGRAM_API_TOKEN is unset.
The v2.0.2 patch diff for src/server/http.ts removes wildcard CORS, changes auth to fail closed, and adds Content-Type enforcement on mutations, which aligns with the advisory remediation details.

The copied PoC artifacts /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_2c389da7/.judge/poc_runs/localhost_api_cors_exposure/run.log and http_probe.json show LEAK_OK:src/server/http.ts, a 204 wildcard-CORS preflight on /learn, a 201 {"ok":true} write response, and the persisted marker node captured in persisted_marker.json.

poc_executions.observed_effect: Unauthenticated requests with an attacker Origin header received Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on graph reads and /learn preflight/write, leaked graph metadata including src/server/http.ts, and persisted the attacker-controlled marker `evohunt cross origin write proof` via /learn.
